task_completion.description: Agent navigated to the CAMEO Chemicals website.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open and displaying the CAMEO Chemicals homepage or search interface (cameochemicals.noaa.gov).

task_completion.description: Agent searched for the required chemicals and viewed their Specific Gravity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The web browser shows a chemical datasheet on CAMEO Chemicals for at least one of the target chemicals, with the 'Specific Gravity' section visible.

task_completion.description: Agent created the required output text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or file manager shows a file named 'spill_layering_results.txt' located in the /home/ga/Desktop/ directory.

task_completion.description: The output file follows the exact requested line formatting.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains exactly 6 lines formatted as 'Layer X: [Chemical Name] - SG: [Value]', with Layer 1 labeled '(Bottom)' and Layer 6 labeled '(Top)'.

task_completion.description: The chemicals are ordered correctly from bottom (highest SG) to top (lowest SG).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file lists the chemicals in this exact order: Layer 1: Carbon Tetrachloride, Layer 2: Chloroform, Layer 3: Carbon Disulfide, Layer 4: Water, Layer 5: Toluene, Layer 6: n-Hexane.

integrity.description: Agent used a graphical web browser to search CAMEO Chemicals.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a standard GUI web browser (like Firefox or Chromium) being used to navigate the site, rather than just terminal curl/wget commands.

integrity.description: The SG values recorded in the text file are authentic and match the expected ranges.
integrity.visual_evidence: The SG values written in the text file fall within the verified ranges provided in the privileged info, indicating the agent actually looked them up rather than hallucinating.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct layering order from bottom to top (Layer 1 to Layer 6) is: 1. Carbon Tetrachloride (SG ~1.59, acceptable range 1.50-1.70), 2. Chloroform (SG ~1.48, acceptable range 1.40-1.56), 3. Carbon Disulfide (SG ~1.26, acceptable range 1.20-1.33), 4. Water (SG 1.00), 5. Toluene (SG ~0.90, acceptable range 0.80-0.92), 6. n-Hexane (SG ~0.66, acceptable range 0.60-0.72). The format must strictly follow 'Layer X: [Name] - SG: [Value]' with Layer 1 having '(Bottom)' and Layer 6 having '(Top)'.
